하지만 이 일의 수익 구조는 사실 꽤 간단해요.

이 영상으로 조회수 50만 회에 950달러를 벌었어요.

10,000 뷰 달성까지 남은 날짜.
그래서,
어떻게 될지 지켜봐야겠네요.

K-드라마 남자들한테 배워야 해.
번창하라,
번창하라!

아,
하지만 봐.

이렇게 나를 바라보면,
난 저절로 웃음이 나.
누구나 이게 사랑인 걸 알 수 있어.
하루 종일 함께 있으면,
질려 버릴 거야.

DTrTp5LDsnh.summaryLine: 자신을 개선하며 진정한 관계를 찾는 이야기
DTrTp5LDsnh.translation:
4년 전,
나는 인생의 의미가 여자친구를 얻는 것이라고 생각했었다.
나는 고등학교 시절을 보낸 늦게 꽃핀 17살 소년이었어
학교 시절 리그 오브 레전드 하며
사교 대신 로맨스 애니메이션을 보며.
그래서 나는 친구에게 여자가 나를 좋아하게 하려면 무엇이 필요한지 물었다
나.
그는 말했다,
자신감.
그래서 나는 머리를 밀었다.
그 불편함의 도약이 내
사회적 불안.
그리고 한 달 동안,
사실 저는 제 모습에 점점 더 편안함을 느끼기 시작했어요
피부.
하지만 그 후 나는 대학에 들어갔다.
아이들이 이런 이야기를 하는 걸 듣기 시작했어,
아,
이 여자가 나랑 사귀고 싶어 한다.
그리고 나는 혼란스러워졌다.
나는 진정한 나 자신이 되고 있었어,
바보 같지만,
바보 같은 나 자신.
그리고 여자들은 단 한 번도 나랑 사귀고 싶다고 말한 적 없어.
그래서 유튜브를 보다가 근육질 남자들이 눈에 들어왔어
남자들이 말하길,
아니,
여자들이 진정으로 원하는 건 상위 1%의 남자다.
그래서 나는 그 다음 해를 최대한 멋지게 보이려고 보냈어,
최대 상태 달성,
지금도 저는 시그마 남성(Sigma male)이 되려고 노력 중입니다.
그리고 이 모든 게 여자들의 관심을 받는 데 확실히 도움이 됐어.
내 인생에서 처음 있는 일이다.
그러나,
관계 없음.
그저 내가 전혀 관심 없을 사람들과의 몇 번의 애매한 관계였을 뿐
친구로 지내요.
그리고 약간의 인정도.
그러던 어느 날,
내 친구가 이 여자를 소개해줬어.
그녀가 귀여워 보였어.
그래서 나는 평소처럼 행동했다.
무심한 태도를 유지했다.
그러나,
우리가 진짜 대화를 시작했을 때,
끔찍한 일이 일어났어.
그녀와 함께할 이상적인 남자로 프레임을 고수하기보다는,
실수로 실수를 저지르고 그녀와 대화를 시작했어
리그 오브 레전드와 헬스장 중독에 대해 몇 시간이고 이야기했지.
그 다음날 아침,
저는 자기계발에 대한 새로운 동기를 느꼈습니다.
그건 단순히 ‘좋아받을 만한 존재’가 되기 위한 싸움이 아니었다.
진정으로 사랑하는 사람과 삶을 함께 쌓아가는 것에 관한 이야기입니다.
함께 시간을 보내는 즐거움을 느꼈습니다.
지금,
그 오랜 세월을 쫓아다닌 이야기라고 생각할지도 모르겠네
자기계발은 중요하지 않았다.
하지만 그건 사실이 아닌 것 같아.
하지만 또한,
여자친구를 만난다고 해서 내 삶이 마법처럼 달라지진 않았어
행복과 의미.
미안해.
난 그냥 네가 싫어.
이미 사랑하던 모든 것들에 더 많은 색채를 더해준 것 같아요.
삶에서 사랑했던 모든 것들.
내 일에는 더 깊은 비전이 생겼다.
여행은 더 다채로워졌습니다.
TFT에는 이제 듀오 파트너가 생겼습니다.
결론은,
마음과 몸을 계속해서 갈고 닦으세요.
미친 듯한 목표를 쫓아라.
누군가 당신을 좋아할 만큼 매력적이 되기 위해서가 아니다.
하지만 언젠가,
당신은 꿈에 그리던 여자를 만날 거예요.
미래의 당신에게,
그때의 당신은 그 최고의 존재가 된 자신을 고마워할 거예요.
그녀를 위한 최고의 동반자가 될 수 있도록.

C6uGU4xxKV_.transcript: Is it possible to ace a final after studying for just one hour? So my rhetorical traditions final is tomorrow, and I have knock-on to class in a month. So we're gonna be doing our exact study method to get the highest grade possible after studying for just one hour. Last time we did this, we got a 95 on our previous final. So even though I'm deviously screwed for this final, I got trust in the method. Now a lot of people ask me, what is this method? It's just a bunch of short-term memory hacks. But I realized one of the most important aspects to studying efficiently, in my opinion, is avoiding I'm-cooked syndrome. It's that feeling that you get while you're studying, and you're just like, I'm cooked, and you mentally tune out. I used to do this thing in high school where I would study 8 hours the night before a test, but I would like, just look at the textbook, and my brain would not even be there. Alright, it is exam day, I am cooked. Fun fact, I actually got points off on my last intern because I filmed a video about it. We just finished... I bullshitted, like, every single essay question. I'm not even kidding. It was like, reference the readings, and I was like, I never read any of them. Like, I think I relied more on contents clues than memory for the exam. Follow to, uh, see what I get.

DLI6U7VRPf0.transcript: If you've been laying in bed scrolling for the past hour and you feel like you just ruined the rest of your day so you have zero motivation, please just give me 60 seconds, I'm going to change your life. I've read over 20 self-help books in the past two years just to learn this one mental model and it helped me go from this physique to this physique. It's also helped me make hundreds of thousands of dollars online and the single reframe that you need to start thinking about your day is that you don't need more motivation, you need more gratitude. So the first thing I do right when I get out of bed, I don't care if I woke up at 7 a.m., started my day early, or I woke up at 11 a.m. and I feel so behind on my day, I will imagine what it would be like if I died. What the fuck, how do you imagine if you died? Like I imagine I just somehow got shot in the head and I was talking to death, I was talking to God. And I'll play out this imaginary conversation where I'm talking to God and I'm like, you know, I probably would've hit the gym one more time, you know, I missed that high of getting a pump. I probably would've posted that one video, why was I so scared? Why did I spend my last hour of my life scrolling on my phone? There was so much more I could've done with life. And what's so beautiful about this practice is that motivation will come organically through a genuine appreciation for the ordinary, mundane things that we generally miss in life. And it creates this urgency when you realize you actually will die one day, but it's really easy to get distracted and not understand the gravity of our mortality when we're literally getting spoon-fed dopamine right to our face from these little metal boxes on our phone. So I highly implore you, even if you're halfway through your day, you feel like your day is cooked, turn off your phone, imagine that you just died. Think about all of the simple shit that you'd probably miss, like the clouds above your head if you were just dead right now. And then you'll realize that you actually have more than enough intrinsic motivation to go out and enjoy life in a healthy way. All right, peace.

DVCHF0YjGHW.transcript: In the past two years, I've helped over 50 people reach over 10,000 followers, and I always see this pattern, where it's around day 60 to 90 of daily posting, they'll start to reach that exponential growth curve. Now, you might think they use some insane hook strategy or content pillars, but in reality, the primary thing that they were able to do is just stay consistent with posting. This is what's actually gonna build your personal brand, not some fucking bullshit strategy that you can mentally masturbate to. So I wanna give you six practical tips to do so. One is a minimum viable video strategy that takes you no more than 40 minutes to make. Two is it doesn't really matter what posting frequency you take, as long as you stick to it. So my preferred methodology is one post a day, no matter what, before I go to bed. I would sometimes start my video at like 1 a.m. and just happen to post it at 4 a.m. Third pro tip, this is a tip I learned from the gym, but when you miss a day, just no matter what, do not miss two days in a row. Our motivation is the lowest when we miss a day, and the last thing you wanna do is beat yourself up on this day where you've kinda fucked up. Life happens, you're gonna miss a day, just don't miss twice. Four, use software and hardware that reduces your friction actively. I see so many people talking about how, oh, I need this camera. Why would you tack on even 30 minutes of extra thought and work that you'd have to put into your videos? There's just more reason for you to not show up. Five is journaling about your why and all of the points of friction that are stopping you from posting. I don't know what compelled me to do this, but I wrote down three specific reasons why I would probably give up posting content while I was in college. First one was I was really scared of my acquaintances from college seeing my video, and then me having to run into them on campus, and being like, yo, I've seen your video, bro. Second one was there was gonna be days where I'd just be tired, and I didn't wanna show up and look tired on camera. The third, I'm going to be uninspiring most days, and I won't have value to provide on most days. And when I really took a step back and just thought about it, I realized that all three of these things could actually be advantages. First one, the acquaintances concept. Well, 90% of college students will never post and never create college content, which has such a high demand, just because they're scared of this one thing. So if I could conquer this one fear, I will stand out instantly. That's what happened. I just crushed it with exam videos. The second thing, looking tired on camera. And the third thing, which is I don't have value to provide. 90% of entrepreneur and business creators, they only talk about their wins. I built an actual dedicated following of people because I specifically made a bunch of videos on those days that I didn't have value or I looked tired. Your flaws are what make you relatable to other people, so don't hide them. And that was a strength that gave me a lot of wind under my sail. So the sixth hack for maintaining this rugged level of consistency then was just rereading this every single morning. Because every single morning you're gonna wake up and then the anxieties of, ah man, this posting thing is probably not gonna work out. You're gonna feel that every single morning when you wake up, even if you feel inspired the night before. Your dream life and the exponential growth that you wanna have in your content and in your business, it's behind a wall of just being able to show up and put shit out there, despite feeling hungover one day, despite having nothing to talk about one day, and just doing it anyways.
